# Verification: #208 — Build review-pr L2 recipe with diff-scoped quality standards, severity taxonomy, and reviewer selection
<!-- Generated by start-feature-planning | Plan sub-agent output -->

**Intent:** Verify that the taxonomy, config, skill, recipe, and ship integration satisfy determinism, L2 budget, bypass-conditional gating, and F1..F9 of `review-pr` and `ship`.

**Constraints:**
- All verification runnable locally on a real test PR in `meridian-os`.
- No hand-editing of compiled SKILL.md — all recipe verification is against the compiled artifact produced by `/create-recipe`.
- Determinism check must be empirical: two back-to-back runs on the same PR.

**Failure Conditions:**
- Any acceptance criterion returns a GAP result.
- Determinism re-run produces differing P1..P4 buckets.
- `ship` with `bypass=false` merges a PR that contains a seeded P1 finding.
- `intent_hash` drift detected in either `ship/SKILL.md` or `review-pr/SKILL.md`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `core/components/memory/standards/pr-severity-taxonomy.md` exists and every row cites a real standard ID with a grep/path pattern.
- [ ] `core/config.yaml` contains `review-pr:` section with all 6 required keys and a `standards.pr-severity-taxonomy` resolution entry.
- [ ] `core/components/skills/quality-check-scoped/SKILL.md` exists, declares diff-bounded input, reads KB from `standards_order`, and emits findings that each cite a standard ID and taxonomy bucket.
- [ ] `core/components/recipes/review-pr/reference/intent.yaml` exists with ≥7 constraints, ≥6 failure conditions, ≥3 scenarios, and the confidence formula documented.
- [ ] `core/components/recipes/review-pr/SKILL.md` present, valid intent_hash matching `sha256(reference/intent.yaml)`, ≤5 domain agents in boundary table, never invokes `/quality-check`.
- [ ] `core/components/recipes/ship/reference/intent.yaml` has updated intent paragraph, new C7, new F7, new S3; `ship/SKILL.md` has fresh intent_hash matching.
- [ ] `.claude/specs/idsd/idsd.md` P13 row reads `✅ COMPLETE — L2`, Dependency Blockers section no longer shows `validator → P13`, new paragraph documents bypass-conditional ship step.
- [ ] `create-pr/reference/intent.yaml` and `create-pr/SKILL.md` byte-identical to pre-change state (no reviewer logic leaked in).
- [ ] `/create-recipe --review review-pr` returns 11/11 PASS (G1..G11).
- [ ] `/create-recipe --review ship` returns 11/11 PASS after re-bake.
- [ ] Running `review-pr` twice on the same PR produces identical P1..P4 buckets (determinism).
- [ ] `ship` with `review-pr.bypass=true` behaves exactly as today (no review gate).
- [ ] `ship` with `review-pr.bypass=false` and a seeded P1 finding halts before `merge-pr`.

## Verification Steps

| Step | Method | Expected Outcome |
|---|---|---|
| V1 Taxonomy shape | `grep -E '^\| [A-Z]+-[0-9]+'` on taxonomy file | Every row begins with real domain standard ID |
| V2 Taxonomy determinism | Feed same diff through `quality-check-scoped` twice | Byte-identical `findings.yaml` (stable ordering) |
| V3 Config schema | `yq` read of `review-pr.*` keys | All 6 keys present with correct defaults |
| V4 Skill contract | Read `quality-check-scoped/SKILL.md` | Declares all required inputs; output schema includes `standard_id`, `severity`, `taxonomy_rule_id` |
| V5 Recipe audit | `/create-recipe --review review-pr` | 11/11 PASS |
| V6 Domain-agent budget | Count unique domain agents in `review-pr/SKILL.md` | ≤5 |
| V7 Ship audit | `/create-recipe --review ship` | 11/11 PASS and intent_hash fresh |
| V8 create-pr untouched | `git diff HEAD~N core/components/recipes/create-pr/` | Empty diff |
| V9 Bypass path | Set `bypass: true`, run `ship` on test branch | Skips review-pr, matches legacy behavior |
| V10 Block-on-P1 path | Set `bypass: false`, seed a P1, run `ship` | Halts before `merge-pr`, PR has review-pr comment + reviewers |
| V11 Low-confidence path | Force `confidence < 0.5`, run `ship` | Halts + requests reviewers even without P1 |
| V12 Reviewer selection | Run `review-pr` on PR with known recent committers | Top 3 match recency-weighted git log; PR author excluded; fallback triggers when <3 |
| V13 F3 guard | Fabricate finding without `standard_id`, re-run | `quality-check-scoped` rejects / skill fails |
| V14 idsd.md | `grep 'P13' .claude/specs/idsd/idsd.md` | P13 marked COMPLETE L2 |
| V15 Sync | `/sync-claude` after all changes | No errors; review-pr + quality-check-scoped deployed |

## Edge Cases

| Scenario | Expected Behavior |
|---|---|
| PR diff touches only docs | `scan_coverage=1`, only DOC-* standards evaluated; findings P3/P4; confidence high; auto-proceed |
| PR diff touches brand-new file with no git history | Reviewer selection falls through to `fallback_reviewers`; if empty → structured failure F8 |
| PR author is only recent committer on all changed paths | Exclude author → 0 candidates → fallback; if fallback empty → fail loud |
| `review-pr.bypass=true` but P1 exists | `ship` ignores review-pr entirely; ships anyway (bypass is explicit) |
| Taxonomy file missing | Pre-flight hard halt with actionable message |
| STM has no prior resolution traces | `precedent_match` defaults to 0.5; logged in evidence |
| Same PR re-run after main-branch update (diff unchanged) | Findings identical; confidence may differ if precedent_match/scan_coverage inputs changed; acceptable |
| PR body has no linked issue and no `spec/intent.yaml` | Step 1 falls back to PR body as intent summary; `intent_alignment` logged as low |
| Review comment already exists from prior run | repo-orchestrator updates existing comment rather than stacking |
| `confidence_threshold` set to 1.0 | Every run requests reviewers — valid escape hatch for maximum caution |
